title: XIFT v1.0 Capability Advertisement (Channel 1)
description: The signed capability advertisement an agent publishes for discovery and serves on Channel 1 (Discovery & Handshake). The extended document is a strict superset of the baseline: every baseline field is present, and the §4.2 fields (channel_capabilities, capability_vector, resource_costs, custodian_eligible, ontology, mcp_servers) are OPTIONAL additions used when the agent participates in Channels 5-7 or interop. Signed with Ed25519 over JCS (RFC 8785) of all fields except capability_signature. Source: xift-1.0-spec-channel-1.md §3, §4.

did.description: Advertising agent's DID. MUST match the resolved DID document (else protocol:channel1:capability_did_mismatch, 101).

version.description: Monotonic advertisement version; increments on each republication.

endpoints.description: Per-channel endpoint URLs (§3). Semantic-channel endpoints (sdr/siea/scs) are OPTIONAL; present only when the agent participates in Channels 5-7.

supported_extensions.description: Envelope extensions this agent processes (§3). Core extensions MUST be recognized; quality/ontology are silently ignorable.

supported_encryption_schemes.description: OPTIONAL. HPKE (or other) suites the agent accepts for the encryption extension.

supported_did_methods.description: DID methods this agent can resolve. A querier whose DID method is absent is rejected with protocol:channel1:did_method_not_accepted (105).

max_inline_size_kb.description: OPTIONAL. Largest inline payload accepted, in KB. Bounded by core §10 payload_inline_size_max (64 KB).

channel_capabilities.description: OPTIONAL (§4.2). Per-channel parameters and feature flags for Channels 5-7.

capability_vector.description: OPTIONAL (§4.3). Versioned Capability Vector(s). A single object, or an array when the agent publishes vectors under multiple embedding models.

governance_constraints.description: Receiver-side baseline constraints exposed at discovery time (§4.4). Hints, not contracts; the receiver's policy engine remains authoritative and MAY be stricter.

resource_costs.description: OPTIONAL (§4.5/§4.6). Soft routing hints; may be stale or inaccurate. Includes billing-aware fields for SDR cost filtering.

estimated_token_cost_per_query.x-xift-note: JCS/RFC 8785 serializes numbers canonically; producers SHOULD emit a stable decimal representation to keep the capability_signature reproducible.

custodian_eligible.description: OPTIONAL (§4.2). Whether this agent can take the Trust Custodian role. Irrelevant to Flow A (no custodian).

ontology.description: OPTIONAL Mechanism-A advertisement of ontology support (§4.2, ADR-XIFT-ONTOLOGY-001). Absent => no ontology support advertised.

mcp_servers.description: OPTIONAL (§4 intro). Interop hints describing MCP servers the agent exposes. Shape is interop-specific; left open.

identity_handshake.description: Handshake entry point (§3, §7). Peers initiate the identity handshake here.

supported_methods.description: Handshake protocols supported (identity-provider-specific, e.g. "iatp-v1"). No overlap with the peer => protocol:channel1:handshake_method_unsupported (105).

expires_at.description: Lifecycle bound; agents MUST refresh before expiry (else protocol:channel1:capability_advertisement_expired, 105).

capability_signature.description: Base64url Ed25519 signature over JCS(advertisement minus this field). Receivers and Custodians MUST verify before using any field (§4.1).

signing_key_id.description: DID URL fragment identifying the key that signed this advertisement.

capability_vector.description: Versioned Capability Vector (§4.3). Implementations MUST publish model and dimensions for embedding compatibility.

bloom_filter.description: Bloom filter over discrete capability tags.
